dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 2616 : Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To require public elementary and middle schools that receive funds under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to obtain parental consent before changing a minor's gender markers, pronouns, or preferred name on any school form or sex-based accommodations, including locker rooms or bathrooms.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids Act.

section.header: Parental consent requirement related to gender markers, pronouns, and preferred names on school forms and sex-based accommodations

subsection.text: As a condition of receiving funds under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 6301 et seq.), a public school that receives funds under such Act shall obtain parental consent before changing a covered student’s—

paragraph.text: gender markers, pronouns, or preferred name on any school form; or
sex-based accommodations, including locker rooms or bathrooms.
The term covered student means a minor who is—

subparagraph.text: a student in any of the middle grades.

paragraph.text: The terms elementary school, middle grades, and parent have the meanings given such terms in section 8101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7801).

section.header: Prohibiting use of ESEA funds to teach gender ideology
section.text: Section 8526 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7906) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraph (7) as paragraph (8); and
by inserting after paragraph (6) the following:

to teach or advance concepts related to gender ideology, as defined in section 2 of Executive Order 14168 (90 Fed. Reg. 8615; relating to defending women from gender ideology extremism and restoring biological truth to the Federal Government); or

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives May 20, 2026.
